metadata.title: Nuclear Energy ≠ Nuclear Bomb | Interactive Experience
metadata.description: An immersive interactive experience to understand the fundamental difference between peaceful nuclear energy and nuclear weapons.
metadata.keywords: nuclear energy, nuclear reactor, nuclear bomb, education, science

cta.title: Knowledge is the First Step
cta.description: Now you know the difference. Share this knowledge with others and contribute to building a clean and sustainable energy future.

footer.credits: An interactive educational experience to spread scientific awareness

myth.title: Nuclear Bomb = Nuclear Energy
myth.subtitle: Both originate from the same idea and foundation—the difference lies only in the method of application, and there is no fundamental difference between them except in form.

weapon.explanation: An image similar to a nuclear bomb
weapon.description: It can destroy and devastate entire cities within seconds, with immense power that cannot be stopped or resisted

reactor.explanation: An image similar to a nuclear reactor
reactor.description: It resembles a nuclear bomb in its impact and can cause the same massive destruction, as it is fundamentally based on the same principle

truth.title: There is a vast difference that cannot be ignored
truth.subtitle: A nuclear bomb is not like a nuclear reactor, and there are very significant differences between them in many areas, most notably:

items.title: Reactions
items.description: Learn how nuclear reactions occur in both reactors and bombs.

items.title: Radiation
items.description: Learn more about the nature of radiation and how it differs between nuclear reactors and nuclear bombs.

items.title: Role
items.description: Explore the role of both nuclear reactors and nuclear bombs and what the difference is between them in purpose

items.title: Mechanism
items.description: Distinguish between myth and reality in how nuclear reactors and nuclear bombs work.

comparison.title: Revealing the Differences
comparison.description: A comprehensive detailed comparison that clearly illustrates the differences and gaps between nuclear reactors and nuclear bombs, in terms of operating method, purpose, control level, and the results produced by each.

slider.title: Detailed Comparison
slider.description: Move the slider to explore the fundamental differences between peaceful and military use of nuclear energy

bomb.description: Occurs in fractions of a second

reactor.description: Can be stopped at any time

reasons.title: Why is the transformation impossible?
reasons.subtitle: Four definitive scientific and engineering reasons

items.title: Low Enrichment
items.description: Uranium in reactors is enriched to only 3-5%, while a bomb needs 90%+ U-235
items.detail: The reaction cannot reach the nuclear explosion stage with this low percentage

items.title: Absence of Instant Critical Mass
items.description: Reactor fuel is distributed in separate and spaced rods, preventing instant critical mass
items.detail: A bomb requires assembling critical mass in fractions of a second

items.title: Counter-Engineering Design
items.description: The reactor is designed to automatically slow the reaction when temperature increases (Negative Feedback Coefficient)
items.detail: Increased heat reduces reaction efficiency instead of increasing it

items.title: Redundant Safety Systems
items.description: Control rods, emergency cooling, and containment buildings prevent any catastrophic scenario
items.detail: Even in the worst accidents, a nuclear explosion does not occur

myths.title: Myths and Facts
myths.description: Click on each card to reveal the scientific truth behind the most famous misconceptions about nuclear energy

labels.knowledgeWeapon: Knowledge is the weapon against unjustified fear

items.myth: Nuclear reactors can explode like a nuclear bomb
items.detail: Uranium concentration in reactors (3-5%) is much lower than required for a nuclear explosion (+90%)
We are exposed to radiation daily from the sun and soil. Reactors are protected by multiple layers of insulation

items.myth: Nuclear waste cannot be disposed of
items.detail: Waste is stored in special containers underground, and its volume is very small compared to coal

items.myth: Chernobyl and Fukushima were nuclear explosions
items.detail: They were steam and hydrogen explosions resulting from human error and natural disasters

items.myth: Nuclear energy is the most dangerous energy source
items.detail: Deaths per terawatt-hour: Nuclear 0.03, Coal 24.6, Oil 18.4

items.myth: Nuclear energy contributes to global warming
items.detail: Nuclear energy does not produce CO2 during operation, similar to solar and wind

myth.title: The nuclear reactor reacts like a nuclear bomb
myth.subtitle: The nuclear reactor reacts in a way similar to a nuclear bomb, but controlling the reactions inside the reactor is very difficult and almost impossible.

truth.title: Not all nuclear reactions are like a nuclear bomb
truth.subtitle: Nuclear reactions differ in their nature, as not all of them lead to an explosion like a nuclear bomb, but many of them occur in a controlled and safe manner.
truth.reactorFission: Fission rate in a nuclear reactor
truth.weaponFission: Fission rate in a nuclear bomb

info.title: Information
info.stableDesc: In a real reactor, control rods absorb neutrons to maintain a stable chain reaction.
info.unstableDesc: Insert control rods to absorb excess neutrons and avoid a meltdown!

explosion.title: Reactor Explosion!
explosion.desc: Control over the nuclear reaction was lost. This is what happens when neutrons are not properly controlled.

causeOfConfusion.barTitle: Reason for confusion: shared physical properties
causeOfConfusion.title: Shared Physical Basis
causeOfConfusion.description: Different nuclear reactions are based on the same physical principles, which makes them similar in general principles despite the difference in results and applications.

energyEquation.title: Einstein's Equation
energyEquation.explanation: Energy = Mass × the square of the speed of light
energyEquation.detail: This equation explains how a very small mass transforms into massive energy in nuclear reactions

einsteinExamples.barTitle: Examples of Einstein's Equation in Nuclear Energy

fission.title: Nuclear Fission
fission.subtitle: Nuclear Fission
fission.description: The process of splitting a heavy atomic nucleus (such as Uranium-235) into two lighter nuclei upon absorbing a neutron, releasing massive energy and additional neutrons.
details: A Uranium-235 nucleus absorbs a neutron
The nucleus becomes unstable and vibrates
It splits into two smaller nuclei + 2-3 new neutrons
A small amount of mass converts to energy (E=mc²)

chain.title: Chain Reaction
chain.subtitle: Chain Reaction
chain.description: The neutrons released from the fission of one nucleus can cause fission in other nuclei, creating a series of reactions.
details: Each neutron can split another nucleus
The reaction doubles exponentially or is controlled

critical.title: Critical Mass
critical.subtitle: Critical Mass
critical.description: The minimum amount of fissile material needed to sustain a self-acting chain reaction without an external neutron source.
details: Depends on the type and purity of the material
Shape and density affect the value

keyPoint.title: Key Point
keyPoint.description: The fundamental difference between a reactor and a bomb lies in the speed of reaction and control: a reactor slows down and controls the reaction to produce sustainable heat, while a bomb accelerates the reaction explosively within fractions of a second.

myth.title: The nuclear reactor can cause a nuclear explosion
myth.subtitle: Operating a nuclear reactor can lead to an explosion similar to a nuclear bomb, and the resulting energy is completely uncontrollable.

timeline.subtitle: This is one of the disasters caused by nuclear reactors, and it resulted in the destruction of the surrounding ecosystem.
timeline.tip: The explosion was exactly like a bomb

timeline.subtitle: Fukushima was no less dangerous than what happened in Chernobyl; it is considered one of the most severe disasters caused by nuclear reactors.
timeline.tip: The explosion was exactly like a bomb

truth.title: A reactor cannot cause a nuclear explosion
truth.subtitle: Scientifically, operating a nuclear reactor cannot cause an explosion like a nuclear bomb; fundamental differences in design and operation make this impossible.

timeline.name: Chernobyl
timeline.cause: Human error during a safety test
timeline.actualEvent: Steam explosion and fire in the reactor core
timeline.keyFact: It was not a nuclear explosion

timeline.name: Fukushima
timeline.cause: Earthquake and tsunami disabled cooling systems
timeline.keyFact: It was not a nuclear explosion

safteySystem.barTitle: The possibility of nuclear disasters happening again is almost impossible with the presence of:

items.title: Multiple Barriers
items.description: 5 barriers between the fuel and the external environment

items.title: Emergency Cooling
items.description: Multiple redundant backup cooling systems

items.title: Automatic Shutdown
items.description: Shuts down automatically upon any malfunction

myth.title: All nuclear radiation is deadly and toxic
myth.subtitle: All nuclear radiation is inherently deadly and toxic, and any exposure to it will inevitably cause direct and severe harm.

truth.title: Did you know: Some types of nuclear radiation are very useful
truth.subtitle: Did you know that certain types of nuclear radiation have beneficial uses in fields such as medicine and industry when properly controlled?

correctingConcept.weapon: Nuclear bomb radiation: harmful and deadly
correctingConcept.reactor: Nuclear reactor radiation: beneficial and safe under controls

items.title: Time
items.description: Reduce exposure time to radiation
items.detail: The less time, the lower the dose

items.title: Distance
items.description: Increase distance from the source
items.detail: Dose decreases with the square of the distance

items.title: Shielding
items.description: Use protective materials

environment.description: Carbon dioxide emissions per kilowatt-hour of electricity produced

emissions.title: High Energy, Low Emissions
emissions.text: Nuclear energy produces electricity at the same emission level as renewable energy, but provides constant power around the clock regardless of weather.

land.title: Small Land Footprint
land.text: One nuclear plant produces energy equivalent to thousands of hectares of solar panels or hundreds of wind turbines.

future.title: Green Future
future.text: Nuclear energy is essential to achieve carbon neutrality. It is the bridge between fossil fuels and a sustainable future.

medicine.title: Medicine
medicine.description: Diagnosis and treatment of diseases using radioactive isotopes

industry.title: Industry
industry.description: Quality inspection and industrial process improvement

research.title: Scientific Research
research.description: Exploring secrets of the universe and matter

desalination.title: Water Desalination
desalination.description: Providing fresh water for arid regions

media.title: Media and Misconceptions
media.subtitle: How Media Outlets Affect Our Understanding
media.description: How the media contributed to forming a distorted image of nuclear energy, and how we can correct these perceptions

influence.title: How does the media affect our perceptions?

items.title: Movies and Series
items.description: Portraying nuclear energy always in a catastrophic light
items.impact: Creates a subconscious link between nuclear and destruction

items.title: News Media
items.description: Focusing on accidents and ignoring achievements
examples: Failure to mention nuclear energy benefits
items.impact: Forming negative public opinion without a scientific basis

items.title: Social Media
items.description: Rapid spread of misinformation

importance.title: Why is correcting misconceptions important?
importance.description: Correct information leads to better decisions at all levels

items.title: Political Decisions
items.description: Unjustified fears lead to inefficient energy policies

items.title: Climate Crisis
items.description: We need all clean energy sources to face climate change

items.title: Sustainable Development
items.description: Nuclear energy is essential to meet growing demand

items.title: Energy Independence
items.description: Reducing reliance on imported fossil fuels

cta.label: Your Role:
cta.text: Share correct information with those around you and contribute to spreading scientific awareness

references.description: Reliable scientific sources we relied on in preparing this educational content

items.description: The official source for information on nuclear energy and nuclear research in the United States

items.description: The leading international organization for peaceful nuclear cooperation and nuclear safety

items.name: Nuclear Threat Initiative
items.description: A non-profit organization working to reduce nuclear threats and enhance global security

note.title: Note on Sources
note.text: All information provided on this site is based on internationally recognized reliable scientific sources. We encourage readers to refer to these sources to dive deeper into understanding nuclear energy and the difference between its peaceful and military uses.

challenges.title: Are you ready ?
challenges.description: Overcome challenges and apply what you have learned in understanding nuclear interactions, and correctly distinguish between facts and myths.

challenges.title: Are you ready for the challenge?
challenges.description: Move through five sequential stages and prove that you can distinguish peaceful nuclear technology from nuclear weapons with confidence.
challenges.completion: Congratulations! You have completed all challenges.

stages.title: Quiz Stage
stages.prompt: What is the main difference between a nuclear reactor and a nuclear bomb?

options.label: They both release energy in exactly the same way.
A reactor releases energy in a controlled way, while a bomb releases it instantly and destructively.
The difference is only the amount of radiation they produce.
A reactor works without uranium, but a bomb does not.

stages.title: Drag & Drop Stage
stages.prompt: Arrange these energy sources from highest carbon emissions to lowest.

stages.title: Matching Stage
stages.prompt: Match each nuclear case to what actually happened.

right.label: A steam explosion followed a badly managed safety test.
Hydrogen explosions happened after a tsunami disabled cooling.
Automatic shutdown and layered barriers are designed to contain risk.

stages.title: Cards Stage
stages.prompt: Select every card that describes beneficial, controlled uses of nuclear radiation.

cards.title: Medical imaging
cards.description: Radioisotopes can help doctors diagnose disease with precision.

cards.title: Cancer therapy
cards.description: Targeted radiation can destroy tumor cells under strict medical control.

cards.title: Explosive blast wave
cards.description: Energy is released instantly to maximize destruction.

cards.title: Mass destruction
cards.description: A chain reaction is accelerated for military devastation.

stages.title: Final Boss
stages.prompt: Use the given values, choose only what you need, build Einstein's equation correctly, then calculate the final energy.
